pi_items.source: setup_azure_devops.ps1 (lines 136-138) explicitly creates a project named 'TailwindTraders' during environment initialization. The project is also referenced in task.json description (line 2) and verified across all other azure_devops_server_env tasks.

In Azure DevOps with the Agile process template, 'Stories' is a standard backlog level that creates a corresponding Kanban board. Verified via Microsoft Learn documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/work-items/guidance/agile-process-workflow?view=azure-devops. The Agile process template (specified in setup_azure_devops.ps1 line 142: 'adcc42ab-9882-485e-a3ed-7678f01f66bc') uses Stories as the default backlog level.

Task description (lines 20-24) specifies final column order: 'New -> Development -> Code Review -> Testing -> Closed'. Default Agile process includes 'New' as the first column state. Verified against verifier.py line 31 which expects this column.
Task description (line 24) states: 'New' and 'Closed' columns should remain with no WIP limits. Verified against verifier.py line 30 which expects itemLimit of 0.
Task description indicates 'New' is mapped to the 'New' state in Azure DevOps. Verified against verifier.py line 30 which expects state_mapping 'New'.
Task description (lines 19-21) states: 'Rename Active to Development (WIP Limit: 3)'. Verified against verifier.py line 31 which expects this column name.
Task description (line 21) specifies: 'Rename Active to Development (WIP Limit: 3)'. Verified against verifier.py line 31 which expects itemLimit of 3.

Task description (line 21) indicates Development column is mapped to 'Active' state. This is the standard state mapping in Agile process. Verified against verifier.py line 31 which expects state_mapping 'Active'.
Task description (lines 22-23) states: 'Add a new column Code Review mapped to Active state (WIP Limit: 2)'. Verified against verifier.py line 32 which expects this column name.
Task description (line 23) specifies: 'Add a new column Code Review (WIP Limit: 2)'. Verified against verifier.py line 32 which expects itemLimit of 2.
Task description (line 22) indicates Code Review column is mapped to 'Active' state (same as Development). Verified against verifier.py line 32 which expects state_mapping 'Active'.
Task description (line 23) states: 'Rename Resolved to Testing (WIP Limit: 3)'. Verified against verifier.py line 33 which expects this column name.
Task description (line 23) specifies: 'Rename Resolved to Testing (WIP Limit: 3)'. Verified against verifier.py line 33 which expects itemLimit of 3.

Task description (line 23) indicates Testing column is mapped to 'Resolved' state. This is the standard state mapping in Agile process. Verified against verifier.py line 33 which expects state_mapping 'Resolved'.

Task description (lines 20-24) specifies final column order includes 'Closed'. Default Agile process includes 'Closed' as the final column state. Verified against verifier.py line 34 which expects this column.
Task description (line 24) states: 'New' and 'Closed' columns should remain with no WIP limits. Verified against verifier.py line 34 which expects itemLimit of 0.
Task description indicates 'Closed' is mapped to the 'Closed' state in Azure DevOps. Verified against verifier.py line 34 which expects state_mapping 'Closed'.
Task description (lines 27-28) states: 'Add a new swimlane named Expedite for urgent work.' Verified against verifier.py line 37 which expects EXPECTED_SWIMLANE to be 'Expedite'.

Task description (line 31) states: 'Add a styling rule (name containing Priority)...' The Priority field is Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority, confirmed in setup_azure_devops.ps1 line 278 and verifier.py line 171. Verified against verifier.py which searches for 'Priority' in filter_str.
Task description (line 32) states: 'cards with Priority = 1'. Verified against verifier.py line 171 which checks 'if "Priority" in filter_str and "1" in filter_str'.

Task description (line 32) states: 'display with a Red background color.' Verified against verifier.py line 38 which defines EXPECTED_RULE_COLOR_TYPE as 'red', and line 193 which checks if RGB red component is dominant (R > G + 50 and R > B + 50).

privileged_info_summary: The customize_kanban_board@1 task operates on synthetic data in a test Azure DevOps environment. The task configures the 'Stories' Kanban board in the 'TailwindTraders' project. All metadata values are verified: columns consist of New, Development, Code Review, Testing, and Closed with WIP limits 0, 3, 2, 3, and 0 respectively. State mappings are New→New, Development→Active, Code Review→Active, Testing→Resolved, Closed→Closed. A swimlane named 'Expedite' must be added. A styling rule must target Priority=1 and apply a red background color (#FF0000 or similar where R > G+50 and R > B+50). The verifier checks column count (6 pts), each column existence and configuration (12 pts per column for name/mapping/limit), column order (10 pts), swimlane presence (14 pts), and styling rule with red color (14 pts total). Minimum passing score is 60 points.
